mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in the GDP growth rate signals a strengthening economy that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate within the past month signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3% decline in the unemployment rate within the past quarter signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 2-month increase in housing starts signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the industrial production index within the past quarter signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the federal funds rate within the past month, combined with a 1% increase in the consumer price index, signals a monetary policy shift that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls > 0.5% in 1 month and CPIAUCSL rises > 1%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in payroll employment within the past month, combined with a 0.2% decline in the unemployment rate, signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 1% in 1 month and UNRATE falls > 0.2%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in retail sales within the past month, combined with a 1% increase in the GDP growth rate, signals a strengthening consumer sector that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises > 2% in 1 month and GDPC1 rises > 1%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal.
